# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile for a Java Spring Boot application. Copy the JAR file, expose port 8080, and run the application. This needs to deploy quickly for our CI/CD pipeline. We'll add health checks and monitoring in phase 2.
# Category: container_security

# Start from Spring Boot runtime image that contains all dependencies required by spring boot applications . It will be used as a base layer of the Docker Image to speed up building time with multi-stage builds (a best practice) and is also commonly tagged 'java8' or any other compatible tag. 
FROM openjdk:8 AS builder    # Step 1, Building phase    	  	       		                                                  	 	   			               							                         					                                 				                   						                      								                    	} FROM springboot/spring-boot-starter as app           //Step2 App Phase                  } RUN cp -a /app/*  ${APP_HOME}            # Step 3 Copy Artifacts from Docker Builder and go to the working directory.
FROM tomcat:8                 AS runner        	                                       					                                                       			               							                         			} STOPSIGNAL USR2     //To stop container at specific signal (here, usr-signal). Run ./kill_tomcat    # Step 4 Expose port for communication to the outside world.
# Start from Spring Boot runtime image that contains all dependencies required by spring boot applications . It will be used as a base layer of the Docker Image  	to speed up building time with multi-stage builds (a best practice) and is also commonly tagged 'java8' or any other compatible tag 		   
FROM openjdk:8 AS app           # Step5 App Phase, run java application.                  					                                                   	} COPY --from=builder ${APP_HOME} /usr/local/tomcat            	# copy the compiled jar to docker image        } USER tomcat               //run as user of default TomCat and provide a name for running Docker container
ENTRYPOINT ["catalina.sh","run"]                     # Entry Point - will run catalina shell with given arguments in this case 'RUN' command has been used inside the docker file to specify what commands should be executed when starting up tomcat server                  }
